replies.msg:
Subway can食法

1. 揀包
最普通白麵包就Italian White
另外可以考慮
Honey Oat (甜啲)
Parmesan Oregano (芝士香草味)

2. 揀餡 睇個人喜好
唔知食咩好就可以叫「sub of the day」 又平又好食

3. 跟住佢會拎個包去焗一焗 所以要等吓

4. 揀配料 即係大家最熟悉嘅this this this
驚其他配料食唔慣可以嗌淨生菜 lettuce only
每次趁啊姐執完菜先再講can I have more XX please 就會幫你塞到啲菜滿曬

5. 揀要邊種汁 又係好睇個人喜好
最大路選擇係Honey mustard ,BBQ同mayonnaise
Chipotle同ranch 呢兩隻都可以一戰
仲有隱藏嘅salt and vinegar 但似乎好淡味
加12蚊可以轉牛油果醬，但其實就係一pat牛油果蓉，冇乜特別

6. 磅水

Z can食法

首先揀主食：飯/拉麵/烏冬
飯可以揀普通飯或者咖哩(辣/唔辣)

之後會放雜菜 包埋嘅
通常係椰菜、翠玉瓜、矮瓜、甘筍之類
揀飲擇食嘅可以叫走菜/少菜

之後揀餸，大約有十幾種：
炸既: 吉列豬扒 南蠻脆炸雞 炸雞翼 吉列魚 炸蝦
唔炸既: 照燒豆腐粒 咖喱牛肉 壽喜燒牛肉 照燒雞扒 豬軟骨 燒鯖魚 蕃茄炒強
(可能有唔同 depends)
一份餐包一餸，兩餸+$10 ，三餸+$16
另外可以加溫泉強

野飲有清茶或者熱齋啡(可以自己加奶)
凍飲都係依兩隻 +$3

單餸飯麵應該$32-$36左右，加餸或者加配料另計
另外可以加嗌coffee menu上面嘅咖啡，照原價另減$10

Dear Colleagues and Students,

The University received a report that a Library staff was preliminarily tested positive for COVID-19 yesterday and the staff is now under medical treatment. The last working day of the staff in the library office is 17 March. Other staff working in the same office are considered as close contacts of the preliminary COVID-19 case and they will follow the instructions from the Centre for Health Protection to take compulsory tests and quarantine. The University has also disinfected the concerned workplace and will provide necessary support to the affected staff members.

Based on the latest information available to us, the concerned staff had lunch in the VA Staff Canteen on 16 March. The Department of Health has sent out reminders to those who were dining at the same venue at similar time to advise them to take voluntary tests. Staff members and students who are asymptomatic but have concerns about the risk of infection or their health condition may consider undergoing an in-house COVID-19 test (please refer to the following hyperlink for further details: https://t.edm.polyu.edu.hk/activities_web/track/click?msgid=e61d9aa5-5527-4ca3-a12e-f7803861b57f&linkid=5f50f8c7598aab00019df30b) or arrange testing at the community testing centers (https://www.communitytest.gov.hk/zh-HK/). The University will continue to monitor the COVID-19 situation and enhance the health and safety measures if deemed necessary.
